ALL SPECIALTIES / DENTAL ASSISTANT / WEST VIRGINIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

West Virginia Dental Assistant Registration

West Virginia Dental Assistant license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Dental Assistant applications in West Virginia run through West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Proof of Training - Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
  • DANB Certification - Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
  • Radiography Certification - Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 1 to 2 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Dental assistants can apply for Registered Dental Assistant through the West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners by registration.

  1. STEP 1

    Create an Account

    Visit the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal and create a new account by clicking 'Register Now' or 'Create an Account'. Complete the registration process, including email verification, to gain access to the application system.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    Complete Online Application

    Log in to the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal and select the application for 'Dental Assistant Registration'. Fill out all required sections of the application completely and accurately.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    Upload Supporting Documentation

    Upload all necessary documents through the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal. This includes proof of being at least 18 years of age, a high school diploma or equivalent, completion of an accredited dental assisting program OR proof of at least 3 months of continuous employment as a dental assistant, and current CPR certification.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    Attest to Knowledge of Rules

    Attest within the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal that you have read and understood the Board's rules and statutes relevant to the practice of dental assisting in West Virginia.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    Pay Application Fee

    Submit the required initial registration fee of $30.00 through the WV Board of Dental Examiners Online Services Portal using a credit/debit card or other accepted payment method.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    Await Board Review

    After submitting your complete application and all required documents and fees via the Online Services Portal, await review and approval from the West Virginia Board of Dental Examiners. You will be notified of your registration status upon completion of the review process.

    Fee: $50 · Timeline: 1 to 2 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
Varies by provider
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$50

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Application page

Renewal

Dental Assistant renewal in West Virginia follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Annually
Renewal fee
$30
Continuing education
2 hours per renewal cycle

Frequently Asked Questions

Does West Virginia require a dental assistant license?

Yes, West Virginia requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in West Virginia?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in West Virginia.

Can I take dental x-rays in West Virginia?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in West Virginia?

State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Available · See board

See board

Renewal

Annually · $30
